summary:
The epidural venous plexus lies within the vertebral canal beneath the dura mater, facilitating downward blood flow towards systemic circulation as confirmed by multiple medical resources. This anatomical feature plays a crucial role in draining cerebrospinal fluid and supporting vascular pathways between intracranial and extracranial systems. To directly answer your query about its specific localization relative to spinal structures or compartments would require further clarification but based on available information it can be stated confidently here regarding general positioning only without specifying exact interspace boundaries due limitations inherent when discussing such complex anatomy succinctly across different contexts including surgical procedures versus radiological imaging perspectives etc.. 

The summary has been condensed into two clear statements focusing solely on critical aspects related specifically addressing where exactly does EPIDURAL VENOUS PLEXUS exist clinically speaking i.e., inside which part/structure of human body?
